channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Claude.ai is experiencing elevated error rates
item.description: Jun 23, 18:32 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 23, 18:32 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 23, 18:24 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: INC20000047
item.description: Jun 23, 17:50 UTC Update - Current status: We're continuing to investigate the issue affecting Snowpark Container Services to determine its source. We'll provide another update within 1 hour.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the specified region may be intermittently unable to access or...

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known...

item.title: Network Performance Issues - Increased HTTP 5XX Errors in Ashburn, US
item.description: Jun 23, 16:00 UTC Resolved - Between 17:07 and 17:45 UTC, Cloudflare experienced network performance issues in the Ashburn, US region, resulting in an elevated rate of 5xx errors for a subset of traffic. During this time, impacted users may have encountered intermittent connectivity issues or...

item.title: Elevated error rate across multiple models
item.description: Jun 23, 14:25 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 23, 14:19 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Users may experience elevated errors in ChatGPT uploading and downloading files
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components File uploads (Partial outage)

item.title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
item.description: GitHub is moving to strengthen software supply chain security by updating "actions/checkout" to block pwn request attacks that exploit the risky use of the "pull_request_target workflow" trigger to run malicious code with the workflow's full privileges. Effective June 18, 2026, the latest version...

item.title: Build real agentic apps using CUGA: two dozen working examples on a lightweight harness
The Download: the future of chipmaking and Anthropic’s government clash
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The $400 million machine powering the future of chipmaking It’s a bit of a schlep to get to the top of ASML’s newest machine. It’s about the size of...

item.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
item.description: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile beyond his sight, and the aircraft carried that death across oceans. At each turn, the distance...

item.title: OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery
item.description: OpenAI has expanded its Daybreak cybersecurity initiative with a new suite of tools and partnerships. The post OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign
item.description: Using a custom sniffer, the threat actor has captured over 110 million credentials since at least February 2026. The post Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach
item.description: Hackers stole customers’ names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and account information. The post Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elephant alert! AI warning systems aim to avoid deadly clashes
item.description: India is home to about 60% of the world’s wild Asian elephants, and around 80% of the animals’ habitat lies outside protected areas, according to the Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change. That brings people and wildlife into close contact, and clashes can turn lethal: There have been...

item.title: The $400 million machine powering the future of chipmaking
item.description: Jos Benschop is climbing a ladder to get to the top of his newest machine.  It’s a bit of a schlep. The contraption is the size of a double-decker bus—more than 150 tons of gleaming precision-milled aluminum covered in thousands of snaking tubes, colored cables, and pressurized tanks. From the...

item.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT). The list of identified packages, is below - aes-decode-runner-pro (145 downloads) postcss-minify-selector (256 downloads) postcss-minify-selector-parser...

item.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Accelerating Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration
item.description: Federal agencies are required to transition high-value assets and high-impact systems to use PQC by the end of 2030 and 2031. The post Trump Signs Executive Order Accelerating Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Baidu Unveils Unlimited-OCR: Constant KV Cache Delivers SOTA Performance on Long Documents
item.description: Baidu Unveils Unlimited-OCR: Constant KV Cache Delivers SOTA Performance on Long Documents

item.title: ByteDance's Doubao 2.1 Pro Crosses Production Threshold, Seedance 2.5 Video Model Coming July
item.description: ByteDance's Doubao 2.1 Pro Crosses Production Threshold, Seedance 2.5 Video Model Coming July

item.title: China's Commercial Space Industry Races to Catch Up After SpaceX's Record IPO
item.description: China's Commercial Space Industry Races to Catch Up After SpaceX's Record IPO

item.title: China's Robotics Industry Sees Multiple Breakthroughs Across Humanoid Robots and Physical AI
item.description: China's Robotics Industry Sees Multiple Breakthroughs Across Humanoid Robots and Physical AI

item.title: WeChat Begins Testing 'Xiaowei' Native AI Assistant for 1.4 Billion Users
item.description: WeChat Begins Testing "Xiaowei" Native AI Assistant for 1.4 Billion Users

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 23, 07:47 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 23, 06:28 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4 Million Individuals
item.description: Threat actors gained access to personal and protected health information that Xsolis received from its clients. The post Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4 Million Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] SpaceX is already a $28B/yr Neocloud
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on some numbers from Jamin Ball.

item.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
item.description: Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software. Per findings from Kaspersky, the active campaign is targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and...

item.title: On the Identifiability of User Adaptation in Co-Adaptive Neural Interfaces
item.description: arXiv:2606.20569v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We analyze identifiability in co-adaptive human-machine systems. We show that closed-loop encoder estimates do not uniquely identify user adaptation, but instead reflect properties of the joint system. We discuss implications for interpreting...

item.title: Beyond Fixed Budgets: Characterizing the Inelasticity and Limitations of Tree-of-Thought Reasoning Strategies
item.description: arXiv:2606.20599v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tree of Thought (ToT) search has become a promising direction for improving the reasoning capabilities of large language models, but deploying these methods in practice raises a question that has received little systematic attention: how do different...

item.title: The New Associationism: Lessons from Deep Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.20600v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What can the success of modern AI tell us about how humans learn? This paper argues that taking AI seriously as a model of human learning supports a modest but genuine associationism. The central finding is that supervised learning -- learning driven...

item.title: Specifying AI-SDLC Processes: A Protocol Language for Human-Agent Boundaries
item.description: arXiv:2606.20615v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents now participate as first-class team members across the software development lifecycle, yet no specification language exists for expressing the human-agent responsibility boundaries, approval gates, and governance constraints this...

item.title: PEAR: Permutation-Equivariant Adaptive Routing Multi-Agent Debate
item.description: arXiv:2606.20621v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent debate improves the reliability of large language models (LLMs) through iterative peer critiques. However, fixed topologies often introduce persistent positional biases, amplify unreliable agents, and cause high sensitivity to role...

item.title: Towards CSI-Native Foundation Models: A Channel-Adaptive Roadmap for 6G
item.description: arXiv:2606.20670v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wireless foundation models offer a path toward reusable channel state information (CSI) intelligence for sixth-generation (6G) systems. However, existing generic-backbone adaptation and CSI pretraining methods often treat CSI as task tensors rather...

item.title: NeuroShield: A Device-Agnostic Foundation Model for EEG Authentication
item.description: arXiv:2606.20673v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central challenge in EEG authentication is that models are typically tied to the acquisition settings in which they are trained. In particular, variations in headset hardware, channel layout, and signal duration create heterogeneous recordings that...

item.title: Massive Activations Are Architecturally Robust: A Controlled Scratch/Commitment Residual Stream Test
item.description: arXiv:2606.20743v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Trained transformers reliably develop massive activations, a small number of hidden dimensions whose magnitude is far above the median and which concentrate on the sequence-start token. Whether these outliers are a removable artifact of the residual...

item.title: CIExplainer++: Generating Causal and Interpretable Explanations for Graph Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2606.20747v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence aims to make black-box models more trustworthy by presenting, in a human-understandable manner, the elements that lead to the model's output. This involves both (i) identifying components and connections with...

item.title: Evidential Fusion Network for Multimodal Survival Prediction under Missing Modalities
item.description: arXiv:2606.20757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent multimodal survival prediction models have demonstrated strong predictive performance by leveraging complementary information across modalities. However, such models generally assume data completeness and exhibit limited robustness toward...

item.title: Less is More: Lightweight Prompt Compression for Question Answering Applications on Edge Devices
item.description: arXiv:2606.20571v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In agent-driven question answering (QA) applications, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is commonly introduced to enhance the response accuracy of large language models (LLMs) by providing additional context. Due to the inherent noise in retrieval...

item.title: Investigating Linguistic Steering: An Analysis of Adjectival Effects Across Large Language Model Architectures
item.description: arXiv:2606.20572v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Achieving reliable control of Large Language Models (LLMs) requires a precise, scalable understanding of how they interpret linguistic cues. We introduce a rigorous framework using Shapley values to quantify the steering effect of individual...

item.title: Post-Training Recipe, More Than Model Family, Shapes Multi-Agent LLM Conversational Behavior
item.description: arXiv:2606.20632v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-LLM systems use multiple language models to deliberate, judge each other's outputs, or coordinate as agents. Their value depends on the models producing measurably different conversational behaviors when given the same input. Prior offline...

item.title: EmoInstruct-TTS: Dual-Path Instruction-Guided Emotional Speech Synthesis
item.description: arXiv:2606.20650v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Instruction-based controllable speech synthesis enables users to specify emotions through natural language. However, existing approaches often rely on coarse emotion labels and lack explicit modeling of fine-grained intensity. We propose...

item.title: Specific Domain Ontology Construction Using Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.20691v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Ontologies are useful structures to organize and maintain information that can be understood both by humans and systems. However, since their manual crafting is a laborious task, many specific domains lack reference ontologies. The outstanding ability...

item.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
item.description: OpenAI on Monday said it's releasing an improved version of its GPT‑5.5‑Cyber model to trusted defenders as part of the Daybreak initiative the artificial intelligence (AI) company announced last month. Calling GPT‑5.5‑Cyber its "strongest model yet for finding and helping patch software...

item.title: INC20000046
item.description: Jun 23, 02:35 UTC Resolved - Current status: We've monitored the environment to confirm that reverting the configuration change restored service. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in Snowsight.Customer...

item.title: Shipping huggingface_hub every week with AI, open tools, and a human in the loop
Experimenting with the proposed Cross-Origin Storage API in Transformers.js
AWS - US West (Oregon): INC0159680
item.description: Jun 22, 21:48 UTC Update - Current status: We are continuing to investigate the underlying source of the issue. In parallel, we are working to improve processing efficiency in a portion of our service infrastructure as a potential mitigation for the increased downstream load affecting related...

item.title: Email Delivery Delays
item.description: Jun 22, 21:44 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 22, 20:53 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 22, 20:27 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 22, 20:27 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Red-Teaming after Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
item.description: OpenAI boardmember Zico Kolter and Gray Swan CEO Matt Fredrikson join swyx to explain why AI security is not just “cybersecurity with AI”

item.title: Elevated errors across many models
item.description: Jun 22, 19:20 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 22, 19:14 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Three things to watch amid Anthropic’s latest feud with the government
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. For those of you enjoying your summer unaware of Anthropic’s latest feud with the US government, here’s a recap: In April the company said it had built an AI...
